text:
GENITOURINARY ONCOLOGY — FOLLOW-UP VISIT
Patient: Robert Delacroix | DOB: [on file] | MRN: [on file]
Attending: [Attending Oncologist]
Visit type: Established patient follow-up

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Mr. Delacroix is a 69-year-old French-Canadian male with metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (mCSPC), stage cT3bN1M1b (bone, pelvic lymph nodes), Gleason 9 (4+5) Grade Group 5, presenting for 12-month follow-up while on continuous ADT plus abiraterone/prednisone.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Mr. Delacroix was initially diagnosed approximately 12 months ago with de novo metastatic prostate cancer (PSA at diagnosis 87.4 ng/mL) with extensive osseous involvement — lumbar spine (L2-L4), bilateral sacroiliac joints, right femoral neck/proximal shaft, left 7th posterior rib — as well as pelvic lymphadenopathy (right obturator 2.1 cm, left external iliac 1.4 cm). Transrectal biopsy confirmed high-grade acinar adenocarcinoma, Gleason 9 (4+5), Grade Group 5, involving all 12 cores bilaterally with perineural invasion and bilateral seminal vesicle involvement on staging CT.

He was initiated on ADT with leuprolide 22.5 mg IM q3 months plus abiraterone (Zytiga) 1000 mg daily with prednisone 5 mg BID approximately 12 months ago for mCSPC. At his 3-month visit, PSA had declined dramatically from 87.4 to 3.2 ng/mL (96% decline), testosterone confirmed castrate at 14 ng/dL. Zoledronic acid was added at that time for bone-directed therapy along with calcium/vitamin D supplementation.

NGS was performed on FFPE biopsy tissue and peripheral blood germline testing at the time of diagnosis. Notably, germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant (c.5946delT, p.Ser1982Argfs*22) was confirmed, with biallelic loss in tumor (somatic LOH). Somatic profile included TP53 p.R248W, homozygous PTEN deletion, CDK12 frameshift, RB1 splice site mutation, PIK3CA p.E545K, and AR amplification (CN 8). MSS, TMB 4 mut/Mb. Genetic counseling referral was placed. Family members have been informed of hereditary implications.

Today at 12 months on regimen, the patient returns for routine follow-up. He reports persistent but manageable hot flashes, ongoing mild fatigue, and new subjective cognitive sluggishness that he describes as "brain fog" — difficulty finding words during conversations and occasional short-term memory lapses. He attributes this partly to poor sleep from nocturnal hot flashes. He denies new bone pain, urinary symptoms, lower extremity weakness or numbness, or fevers. He has been adherent to oral abiraterone and prednisone, confirming he takes Zytiga on an empty stomach at the same time each day.

Bone scan performed recently (interval ~18 months from diagnosis, ~6 months prior to today's visit) showed stable osseous metastases without new lesions. A repeat bone scan was performed approximately 2 weeks ago at the 12-month mark — results reviewed today (see imaging below).

PSA trend:
- Diagnosis: 87.4 ng/mL
- Month 3: 3.2 ng/mL
- Month 6: 1.9 ng/mL
- Month 9 (nadir): 0.8 ng/mL
- Month 12 (today): 1.1 ng/mL

The PSA has risen slightly from nadir (0.8 to 1.1 ng/mL), representing approximately a 37% increase above nadir, though in absolute terms quite modest (0.3 ng/mL absolute rise). This does not yet fulfill PCWG3 criteria for biochemical progression (requires two rises each ≥25% above nadir AND ≥2 ng/mL above nadir). Will monitor closely.

Zoledronic acid most recent dose administered 6 weeks ago (cycle 4); next dose scheduled in 6 weeks. He is tolerating bone-directed therapy without issues — no jaw symptoms, no musculoskeletal adverse effects reported.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
- Constitutional: Fatigue grade 1-2, stable; no fevers, chills; weight stable (reports approximately same weight as last visit).
- Neurologic: Cognitive sluggishness as above; denies headache, focal weakness, paresthesias, bowel or bladder incontinence.
- Musculoskeletal: Known back pain from osseous metastases, stable, managed with scheduled acetaminophen; no new focal bone pain; no fractures.
- GI: Mild constipation managed with psyllium fiber; no nausea, vomiting, diarrhea; appetite intact.
- Cardiovascular: Hot flashes grade 2, persistent; lower extremity swelling mild and stable; no chest pain, palpitations, dyspnea at rest.
- GU: Voiding without difficulty; no hematuria.
- Endocrine/metabolic: Known hot flashes on ADT; no new symptoms of adrenal insufficiency (the patient is on prednisone 5 mg BID which provides glucocorticoid coverage).
- Psychiatric: Mood somewhat low; patient notes reduced libido/sexual function (expected on ADT) and reports some emotional flatness; discussed with wife who accompanied him today.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
Vital signs: BP 128/76 mmHg, HR 68 bpm, RR 16, Temp 37.0°C, SpO2 97% on room air. Weight 88 kg (down 1 kg from last visit). ECOG Performance Status: 1.

General: Well-appearing elderly male in no acute distress. Alert and oriented x3. Mildly fatigued appearance.

HEENT: No lymphadenopathy in cervical, supraclavicular, or axillary regions on palpation. Oral mucosa moist without lesions. No scalp tenderness.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs. No JVD. 1+ bilateral lower extremity pitting edema to mid-shin, unchanged from prior visit.

Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No adventitious sounds.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly or splenomegaly palpated. No abdominal masses. Bowel sounds normal.

Musculoskeletal/Spine: Midline lumbar spine tenderness on palpation at L2-L4, unchanged. No focal thoracic spine tenderness today. No point tenderness over right hip/femur at rest. Full range of motion of upper extremities. Gait steady.

Neurologic: Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Strength 5/5 bilateral upper and lower extremities. No focal neurologic deficits. Sensation intact. Reflexes symmetric 2+. Perianal tone not formally tested today (no new lower extremity symptoms).

Dermatologic: No rash. Mild facial flushing consistent with hot flashes. Gynecomastia: trace, unchanged.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS
PSA: 1.1 ng/mL (prior nadir 0.8 ng/mL; modest rise above nadir)
Testosterone: 11 ng/dL (confirmed castrate, <20 ng/dL threshold)
CBC: WBC 6.0 x10^9/L, Hemoglobin 12.8 g/dL, Platelets 224 x10^9/L — mild anemia, stable (ADT-related, baseline)
BMP: Na 139, K 4.0, Cr 1.0 mg/dL, eGFR ~72 mL/min; BUN 18; glucose 98
LFTs: AST 28 U/L, ALT 32 U/L, Alk Phos 148 U/L (mildly elevated, consistent with known osseous metastases, stable), total bilirubin 0.6 mg/dL — no hepatotoxicity from abiraterone
Lipid panel: LDL 112 mg/dL, HDL 41 mg/dL, TG 168 mg/dL — mild dyslipidemia on ADT, previously noted
HbA1c: 5.9% (pre-diabetic range; patient on chronic corticosteroids — monitoring ongoing)
Vitamin D (25-OH): 42 ng/mL (adequate on supplementation)
PTH: 32 pg/mL (normal)

---

IMAGING RESULTS
Technetium-99m Bone Scan (obtained approximately 2 weeks ago):
Previously described areas of increased radiotracer uptake in lumbar spine (L2-L4), bilateral sacroiliac joints, right femoral neck and proximal shaft, and left 7th posterior rib are stable without significant interval change. No new foci of abnormal tracer uptake identified throughout the skeleton. No superscan pattern. Soft tissue uptake unremarkable. Overall impression: Stable skeletal metastatic disease burden compared to initial diagnosis bone scan.

Prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (obtained at diagnosis): Showed locally advanced primary tumor, pelvic lymphadenopathy, osseous lesions; no visceral metastases. Repeat CT is not indicated at this visit given stable clinical picture; will plan repeat imaging if PSA rises further.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (mCSPC), Gleason 9, BRCA2 germline mutant, on ADT + Zytiga/prednisone x 12 months:
   - Disease remains well-controlled on current regimen. PSA nadir of 0.8 ng/mL at 9 months; slight rise to 1.1 ng/mL this visit (0.3 ng/mL absolute, ~37% above nadir). Does NOT meet PCWG3 criteria for PSA progression at this time — requires two consecutive rises each ≥25% above nadir AND absolute PSA ≥2 ng/mL. Will continue to monitor closely.
   - Bone scan stable — no new osseous lesions.
   - Will continue ADT (leuprolide 22.5 mg IM q3 months; next injection due in ~8 weeks — scheduled in nursing), abiraterone 1000 mg PO daily on empty stomach, prednisone 5 mg BID.
   - Testosterone to be rechecked at next visit; confirm sustained castration.
   - Repeat PSA in 8 weeks. If PSA continues to rise and approaches criteria for biochemical progression, will discuss transition to castration-resistant phase and plan next-line therapy. Given germline BRCA2 mutation (with tumor biallelic loss), PARP inhibitor (olaparib/Lynparza) will be an important future option — timing and sequencing to be revisited when progression confirmed.

2. Bone-directed therapy:
   - Zoledronic acid 4 mg IV q3 months — continue. Next dose in approximately 6 weeks.
   - Calcium 1200 mg daily + Vitamin D 1000 IU daily — continue. Vitamin D level adequate at 42 ng/mL.
   - Dental hygiene reminder: patient instructed to maintain regular dental visits and avoid invasive dental procedures if possible without prior oncology notification (osteonecrosis of the jaw precaution on bisphosphonate therapy).

3. ADT-related toxicities:
   - Hot flashes (grade 2): Discussed venlafaxine vs. gabapentin for symptom management. Patient prefers to defer pharmacologic management for now; will reassess next visit. Reviewed behavioral strategies (cool environment at night, loose clothing, fans).
   - Fatigue (grade 1-2): Encouraged continued moderate exercise. Referred to outpatient physical therapy for structured aerobic conditioning program previously discussed.
   - Cognitive changes ("brain fog"): Likely multifactorial — ADT-related hypogonadism, sleep disruption from hot flashes, possible mild depression. Neuropsych referral considered but deferred for now; suggested sleep hygiene measures. Will screen for depression formally at next visit using PHQ-9.
   - Bilateral lower extremity edema (grade 1): Stable, mild. Recommend compression stockings. Ensure not corticosteroid-related fluid retention as well. No cardiac workup warranted at this time.
   - Anemia (Hgb 12.8 g/dL): Mild, consistent with ADT-related erythropoietic suppression. No intervention required. Monitor.
   - Pre-diabetic HbA1c on chronic prednisone: Counseled on dietary modifications, blood glucose monitoring if symptomatic. Consider endocrinology referral if worsens.
   - Bone density: Repeat DEXA scan recommended (annual on ADT with bisphosphonate); to be scheduled.

4. Germline BRCA2 counseling:
   - Patient previously referred to genetic counseling following identification of germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant (c.5946delT). Confirmed patient attended session. Family cascade testing discussed with adult children. Will document in chart.

5. Cardiovascular risk on ADT:
   - Blood pressure well-controlled today without antihypertensive medications. Lipid profile showing mild dyslipidemia; discussed with primary care physician at last primary care visit per patient report. Continue to monitor.

6. Follow-up:
   - Return to clinic in 8 weeks for repeat PSA, testosterone, CBC, LFTs, BMP.
   - If PSA rises to meet progression criteria on next assessment, will advance discussion of next-line therapy planning.
   - Patient verbalized understanding of the plan. Wife present and questions addressed. Patient remains ECOG PS 1 and functional at home.

Time spent with patient and family: approximately 40 minutes, greater than 50% spent in counseling and coordination of care.

Electronically signed: [Attending Oncologist, MD]
Department of Medical Oncology / Genitourinary Oncology
[Institution on file]
